slides.title: Introduction to Machine Learning
slides.subtitle: From Theory to Practice
slides.description: A comprehensive introduction to the fundamental concepts, algorithms, and applications of machine learning.

slides.title: Learning Objectives
bullets: Implement key ML algorithms from scratch
Evaluate model performance with proper metrics

slides.title: ML Landscape Overview
slides.description: Machine learning encompasses a broad spectrum of techniques, each suited to different types of problems and data structures.
highlights: Reinforcement learning powers game AI and robotics
slides.code:
# Machine Learning
## Supervised
### Classification
#### Decision Trees
#### SVM
#### Neural Networks
### Regression
#### Linear
#### Polynomial
## Unsupervised
### Clustering
#### K-Means
#### DBSCAN
### Dimensionality Reduction
#### PCA
#### t-SNE
## Reinforcement
### Q-Learning
### Policy Gradient

slides.title: The ML Pipeline
slides.description: Every machine learning project follows a systematic pipeline from raw data to deployed model. Understanding this flow is critical for reproducible results.
highlights: Feature engineering is often the key differentiator
Monitoring prevents model drift in production
slides.code:
graph LR
  A[Raw Data] --> B[Clean & Preprocess]
  B --> C[Feature Engineering]
  C --> D[Train Model]
  D --> E{Evaluate}
  E -->|Good| F[Deploy]
  E -->|Poor| C
  F --> G[Monitor]

slides.title: Core Algorithms
slides.description: These six algorithms form the foundation of machine learning. Mastering them provides the toolkit to solve most real-world problems.
highlights: Start simple — linear models are often sufficient
Random Forests are the 'Swiss Army knife' of ML

slides.title: Model Performance Comparison
slides.description: We benchmarked five popular algorithms on the CIFAR-10 image classification dataset. Deep learning models significantly outperform traditional approaches on complex visual tasks.

slides.title: When to Use Which Algorithm
slides.description: Choosing the right algorithm depends on your data characteristics, performance requirements, and interpretability needs.
highlights: Always start with the simplest model that could work
Ensemble methods often win Kaggle competitions

slides.title: This Week's Lab Assignment
slides.description: In this hands-on lab, you'll implement a complete ML pipeline from data loading to model evaluation using scikit-learn.

slides.quote: All models are wrong, but some are useful.
